famous quote:
"God will not be used as a convenience. Men or nations who think they can revive the Faith in order to make a good society might just as well think they can use the stairs of heaven as a shortcut to the nearest chemist's shop."
-- C.S. Lewis
Interview Text:
Will:

(part 8)

Fasting more often helps too. He fasts from food sometimes. He's fasted from coffee a few times. He's fasted from political news, twitter, and facebook. That's all been helpful. The moral is just to make less room for distractions and more room for God, physically and in your schedule.

Then Will added it's important to pray and study the Bible. He found reading it straight through was valuable, especially when partnering it with reading a commentary that would help explain some of the issues that bothered him. But do more. Study it with word search programs and other tools. Go find every verse you can about the topic you are seeking direction on, or just curious about. Treat it like the manual for everything important we do. Not just good principles.

He said the voice of God will never frighten you. It won't be unpleasant. The fruit, the results of God's voice, is always positive. It might remind you of negative things, but you will not feel negative about hearing those things.
Music Video Instructions: Imagine if God's love for us, and ours for him was like this.
